discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape driven by semis (SMH +4.9%, AVGO +4.2%, NVDA +1.8%) on the AMD/Intel/Micron memory rally narrative, with QQQ +2.3% but DIA flat — narrow tech-led leadership. VIX subdued at 17. Mid-cap action dominated by earnings reactions; several large gappers (MXL, SYNA, PENG) tied to semi sympathy on top of prints.

notable_index_moves: SMH +4.9% / XLK +3.4% — memory & CPU rotation per CNBC piece
Spread between QQQ (+2.3%) and DIA (+0.0%) is unusually wide; narrow leadership
Fed-cut odds fading per jobs report — duration-sensitive utilities weak

discoveries.name: Fluor Corporation
discoveries.setup: Earnings reaction in lumpy E&C name with elevated 5-day drawdown
discoveries.thesis: Fluor is down 15% on Q1 print; CNBC framed it as a pullback opportunity, suggesting the print wasn't a thesis-breaker but rather a guide/timing miss typical of E&C. Five-day -18% means most of the damage is already in. Engineering & construction earnings frequently swing on project timing and one-offs (NuScale stake mark, segment margins) rather than secular issues. The 8-K is just 2.02 + 5.07 (annual meeting vote) — no impairment, no restructuring, no management change.
discoveries.what_confirms: Transcript shows backlog growth intact and the miss tied to project timing or NuScale equity mark; sell-side maintains estimates.
discoveries.what_kills: Backlog declining, large project write-downs disclosed in Q, or segment margin guide cut materially — would justify the magnitude and more.
discoveries.what_to_learn: E&C earnings drops of 10-15% on no-impairment 8-Ks frequently overshoot because project-timing noise gets extrapolated; the absence of 2.05/2.06 codes is a meaningful tell.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K shows only 2.02 (earnings) and 5.07 (shareholder vote) — no impairment/restructuring codes, suggesting magnitude is sentiment-driven not structural.
research_pointers: Read transcript for backlog, segment margin trajectory, and NuScale (SMR) equity mark contribution
Compare Q1 EPS/revenue vs consensus and check if FY guide was lowered or reaffirmed
Check if the 15% drop tracks magnitude of FLR's last few earnings reactions or is an outlier

discoveries.name: BILL Holdings, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Earnings rally despite a 2.05 restructuring item in the 8-K
discoveries.thesis: BILL is up 11% on a print that includes a 2.05 (exit/disposal costs) item. Normally 2.05 is a drag, but when paired with a positive stock reaction it usually means the market is rewarding the cost-cut/right-sizing story over the charge itself — classic 'kitchen sink + better core' setup. BILL has been a battleground/short name; a restructuring narrative could be re-rating fuel. Risk is the rally fades once people parse the charge magnitude vs. forward op-margin guide.
discoveries.what_confirms: Restructuring is paired with raised FY operating margin guidance and TPV/take-rate stabilization on the call.
discoveries.what_kills: Restructuring charge is large with no offsetting margin commitment; revenue guide cut. In that case the 11% pop fades.
discoveries.what_to_learn: 2.05 codes paired with positive price action almost always reflect a 'cost discipline rewarded' narrative — confirm by checking whether margin guide was raised in the press release.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K combines 2.02 (earnings) with 2.05 (exit/disposal costs) and 7.01 (FD) — restructuring being celebrated rather than punished.
research_pointers: Quantify charge size vs run-rate cost base; is this real or cosmetic?
Check FY operating margin and FCF guide changes
Short interest and squeeze potential — BILL has historically been a high-SI name

discoveries.name: Globus Medical, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Continuation earnings drop in medtech — already down 14.7% over 5 days
discoveries.thesis: GMED is down 8% on the print after already being down 14.7% over 5 days, suggesting the market knew something was coming and the report confirmed it. With only 2.02 in the 8-K (no impairment, no management change), the driver is likely guide cut or competitive concerns (NuVasive integration, robotics share). The cumulative 23% drawdown in a defensive medtech is more interesting than today's 8% in isolation — could be approaching washout or could mark a structural break.
discoveries.what_confirms: Guide cut tied to specific competitive losses (e.g., spine pricing) or integration friction — would mean more downside.
discoveries.what_kills: Print shows revenue beat with one-time margin pressure; would suggest forced-seller dynamics and a likely bounce.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Clean 2.02-only 8-K with -14.7% pre-print drift suggests information leakage or institutional positioning ahead of a known soft quarter.
research_pointers: NuVasive integration progress and revenue synergy realization
Spine market share data vs SYK, ZBH; robotics (ExcelsiusGPS) traction
Check if FY guide was cut and by how much vs Street

discoveries.name: Oshkosh Corporation
discoveries.setup: Earnings drop in cyclical heavy-equipment maker against a tariff-fear tape
discoveries.thesis: OSK -9.6% on Q1 print with only 2.02 + 5.07 in the 8-K (no impairment, no management change). Coming on the same day as Trump's renewed EU tariff threats, an industrial with European exposure and access-equipment cyclicality is a natural target. Cumulative -10.9% over 5 days. Without granular guide info, hard to say if 9.6% is calibrated, but absence of structural codes suggests it's a guide/margin issue rather than a thesis-break.
discoveries.what_confirms: Postal vehicle (NGDV) program slippage or USPS funding concerns called out; FY EPS guide cut materially.
discoveries.what_kills: Backlog reaffirmed, NGDV on track, miss tied to Q1 mix only — magnitude becomes harder to defend.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Two separate 8-Ks: one for shareholder vote (5.07) and one for earnings (2.02) — no impairment or restructuring items.
research_pointers: NGDV (next-gen postal vehicle) program economics and timeline
Access equipment (JLG) backlog vs prior quarter
Tariff exposure to EU and how guide treats it

discoveries.name: MaxLinear, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Sympathy-driven semi rally with no company catalyst — volume only 1.16x average
discoveries.thesis: MXL +21% with NO 8-K and only 1.16x volume is a tell — this is a pure sympathy move on the AMD/Intel/Micron memory/CPU rotation, possibly amplified by short covering (MXL has been a battleground name with prior infrastructure overhang). The low volume multiple is the key warning: a 21% move on barely-elevated volume = thin-tape squeeze, not real institutional accumulation. 5-day +29% means it's already extended.
discoveries.what_confirms: Short interest data shows compression; or a supply chain / design-win leak appears.
discoveries.what_kills: Volume stays low into next session and price reverses — confirms squeeze-not-reposition. Also: an analyst note pointing out MXL has minimal direct exposure to the memory/CPU theme would be damaging.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Big % moves with sub-1.5x volume in semis during sector squeezes are usually short-covering in low-float / high-SI names — the lack of volume relative to the move is the diagnostic, not the move size.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals plus volume_multiple of only 1.16x against a 21% move — classic low-volume sympathy/squeeze signature.
research_pointers: MXL short interest and days-to-cover most recent reading
Whether MXL has any genuine memory/AI infrastructure exposure or this is pure beta
Check options activity for unusual call buying that might explain the move

discoveries.name: Penguin Solutions, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Memory-adjacent sympathy mover with no company catalyst, already +41% over 5 days
discoveries.thesis: PENG (formerly SMART Global) is a memory/storage solutions company — direct beneficiary of the memory chip rally narrative covered in CNBC. No 8-K, but 5-day +41% indicates the market has been actively repricing it on the Micron-led memory shortage thesis. Today's 13.5% is continuation, not initiation. Risk: it's already extended and may be late-stage in this rotation.
discoveries.what_confirms: Memory pricing data continues higher next week; PENG-specific contract win disclosed.
discoveries.what_kills: Memory rally rolls over (Micron tops out); PENG retraces faster than peers given its smaller size.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals + 41% 5-day move tracks Micron's parabolic rally noted in CNBC — clear sector beta rather than idiosyncratic news.
research_pointers: PENG's actual memory/HBM exposure as % of revenue vs services/SI work
Upcoming earnings date — if close, fade risk into print is high after this run
Compare relative move vs MU peer group to see if late or early

discoveries.name: Texas Roadhouse, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Earnings beat in a defensive restaurant compounder
discoveries.thesis: TXRH +12% on print with 2.02 + 8.01 8-K (8.01 likely a dividend/buyback announcement based on past pattern). TXRH is a quality compounder that has been pressured on consumer/inflation fears; a clear comps beat plus capital return action would justify a 10%+ rerate, especially as a relative-strength rotation candidate vs. discretionary peers. Volume 2.45x supports real institutional buying. Inspire Brands IPO filing today reinforces restaurant-sector attention.
discoveries.what_confirms: Same-store sales beat, traffic positive, FY guide raised; 8.01 is a dividend hike or buyback authorization.
discoveries.what_kills: Beat is purely pricing-driven with negative traffic; margin guide softens for H2.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Quality restaurant compounders with 2.02 + 8.01 8-Ks (where 8.01 = capital return) frequently sustain post-print gains because the pairing signals confidence rather than one-time beat.
discoveries.catalyst: Q1 2026 earnings + likely capital return announcement
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K pairs 2.02 with 8.01 (other events) — historically TXRH uses 8.01 for dividend announcements; explains both magnitude and conviction in the move.
research_pointers: Check 8.01 exhibit content — dividend hike size or buyback authorization
Q1 same-store sales: traffic vs price decomposition
Beef cost commentary and FY margin guide

discoveries.name: LifeStance Health Group
discoveries.setup: Earnings drop in mental health roll-up with FD disclosure
discoveries.thesis: LFST -13% with 2.02 + 7.01 + 9.01. The 7.01 (Reg FD) is what's interesting — when paired with earnings it typically contains either a strategic update, guide change, or M&A-related disclosure. For a roll-up like LFST that's been struggling with clinician retention and unit economics, the addition of FD content suggests something material beyond the print. Magnitude is large but not extreme for this name's history of volatility.
discoveries.what_confirms: FD disclosure contains FY guide cut, clinician retention deterioration, or payer rate pressure detail.
discoveries.what_kills: FD disclosure is a positive strategic update (e.g., new payer contract) that the market mis-read, suggesting a snap-back is possible.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 2.02 + 7.01 combo means there's substantive content beyond the press release — drives the asymmetry of needing to read the FD exhibit.
research_pointers: Read the 7.01 exhibit specifically — what was disclosed
Visit volume and clinician headcount trends
Adjusted EBITDA path and FCF inflection timing

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Memory/CPU rotation narrative (Micron +30% week, AMD/Intel surge) — investors rotating from NVDA-centric AI exposure to broader compute
Inspire Brands (Dunkin' parent) confidentially files for IPO — restaurant sector spotlight

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump threatened 'much higher' EU tariffs if no deal by July 4 deadline (via CNBC, not direct Truth Social)
trump_signals.note: Industrials with EU/global supply chain exposure already weak today; tariff overhang reinforces the down moves but isn't the primary driver — earnings prints are. Worth monitoring whether industrials with EU revenue see continued pressure regardless of fundamentals.

ai_announcements.headline: Cloudflare says AI made 1,100 jobs obsolete, even as revenue hit a record high
ai_announcements.summary: Cloudflare CEO Matthew Prince attributes layoffs of 1,100 support roles to AI efficiency gains. Signals a broader narrative about AI displacing customer support / back-office labor at scale.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: LifeStance is a mental health clinical care provider; the move is almost certainly driven by its 5/7 earnings 8-K, not AI displacement. AI could actually help LifeStance by automating intake/scheduling/notes (lower SG&A).
research_pointers: Read LFST Q1 print and guide; mental health utilization trends
Check if any commentary mentions AI scribe/intake competition
This move is earnings-driven, not the Cloudflare AI headline

ai_announcements.headline: OpenAI launches new voice intelligence features in its API
ai_announcements.summary: OpenAI rolling out voice features pitched at customer service, education, creator platforms.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: BILL move is earnings + restructuring (8-K item 2.05) driven, not voice AI. Voice AI is tangential to AP/AR automation.
research_pointers: BILL Q1 results and restructuring details are the actual catalyst
Not an AI-headline-driven move

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic and OpenAI announce new joint ventures targeting enterprise AI deployment; SAP buys Prior Labs for $1B
ai_announcements.summary: Enterprise AI gold rush: Anthropic + OpenAI rolling out enterprise deployment partnerships, SAP acquiring Prior Labs. Implies pressure on enterprise SaaS incumbents and elevated M&A premia for AI startups. NOTE: This is an Anthropic-related call; flagging for bias review. Per instructions, leaning toward consensus market view that big-foundation-model enterprise pushes are genuine threats to mid-cap SaaS.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Move is dominated by 5/7 earnings 8-K (beat/guide raise likely given +15% on 2.6x volume). Long-term, foundation-model enterprise pushes still threaten Dropbox's file-collaboration moat, but today's print is the catalyst.
research_pointers: DBX Q1 print: Dash AI traction, FCF, buyback
Anthropic/OpenAI enterprise JV is a long-tail risk, not today's driver
ANTHROPIC-RELATED FLAG: bias-check this call separately

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Earnings-driven decline, not AI. CarGurus marketplace network effects intact.
research_pointers: Read CARG Q1 results and dealer count trends

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Today's tape is overwhelmingly Q1 earnings driven (May 7-8 reporting cluster: WSC, MODG/CALY, NSIT, SYNA, FLR, GMED, OSK, KMT, BILL, DBX, TXRH, WMG, CARG, LFST, AGO, PTCT). The AI announcements (Cloudflare layoffs, OpenAI voice API, Anthropic/OpenAI enterprise JVs) are not the proximate driver of any of the listed moves. No clean AI-overreaction setups identified. MXL, SYNA, PENG semis moves may have AI-infra tailwind but absent specific announcement linkage, not actionable here. Quiet night for the Michael playbook on AI-mispricing trades.
